name: Energy-Thermo
domain: Heat transfer, entropy, thermodynamic potentials, irreversibility
core_insight: Energy is conserved but quality degrades. Entropy always increases in isolated systems — this is time's arrow. Efficiency is bounded by the temperature ratio of hot and cold reservoirs. Heat diffuses; it cannot spontaneously concentrate.

key_equations.name: Second Law (Entropy)
key_equations.why: Entropy cannot decrease in isolation — the universe's ratchet that defines past from future

key_equations.name: First Law of Thermodynamics
key_equations.why: Energy bookkeeping: internal energy changes by heat in minus work done

key_equations.name: Carnot Efficiency
key_equations.why: Maximum work extractable from a heat engine — depends only on temperature ratio

seed_prompt: △≈ Which direction does entropy flow? Find the temperature gradient — that is where work lives.

fourth_equation.name: Heat Equation
fourth_equation.why: Temperature diffuses from hot to cold at a rate proportional to the Laplacian — spatial curvature of temperature drives temporal change

explore_paths.question: Why does time only go forward in thermodynamics when all other physical laws are time-reversible?
explore_paths.why: Transformation and phase transitions are time-symmetric at the micro level; the arrow of time is an emergent statistical phenomenon

explore_paths.question: Is information the same thing as thermodynamic entropy?
explore_paths.why: Landauer's principle and Maxwell's Demon connect erasing information to heat generation — entropy in F05 and entropy in F03 are the same quantity

explore_paths.question: What happens to thermodynamics far from equilibrium?
explore_paths.why: Prigogine showed dissipative structures (life, convection cells) self-organize far from equilibrium — emergence happens in the thermodynamic danger zone

explore_paths.question: How does energy constrain life's complexity?
explore_paths.why: Metabolic scaling laws (Kleiber's law: metabolic rate ∝ mass^3/4) are thermodynamic constraints on biological organization

open_questions.question: Can Maxwell's Demon violate the Second Law using information?
open_questions.tension: Maxwell's Demon sorts fast and slow molecules using information, apparently decreasing entropy for free. Szilard (1929) showed the demon must erase its memory, costing kT ln2 of entropy per bit — exactly compensating. Landauer's principle formalizes this. But some argue quantum versions of the demon might evade the argument. The debate about whether information is truly physical is still alive.
open_questions.curiosity_hook: The question is whether intelligence can defeat entropy. Every answer has enormous implications — for computation, for life, for the nature of information itself.

open_questions.question: Is there a Fourth Law of Thermodynamics governing dissipative structures and life?
open_questions.tension: Prigogine (Nobel 1977) proposed that systems far from equilibrium self-organize to maximize entropy production. Kauffman and others have extended this. But there is no consensus formulation of such a law, and critics argue 'maximum entropy production' is not universally valid.
open_questions.curiosity_hook: If a fourth law exists, it would explain *why* life spontaneously appears and organizes — thermodynamics would demand it. This would make life not a miracle but an inevitability.

open_questions.question: Is energy conserved in an expanding universe?
open_questions.tension: In general relativity, the concept of global energy conservation is not straightforwardly defined. The energy of photons red-shifts as the universe expands — where does that energy go? Sean Carroll argues it simply isn't conserved at cosmological scales. Others disagree.
open_questions.curiosity_hook: The most fundamental conservation law in physics may only hold locally. The universe at large may not obey the rules we rely on everywhere else.

cross_domain_bridges.manifestation: Life is a thermodynamic engine running on chemical potential gradients; all metabolism is entropy management
cross_domain_bridges.example: ATP synthase is a literal rotary motor harvesting the proton gradient — a biological heat engine

cross_domain_bridges.manifestation: Economic value degrades like energy; 'entropy' in supply chains is waste, friction, and inefficiency
cross_domain_bridges.example: Exergy economics tracks the quality of energy (not just quantity) through industrial systems

cross_domain_bridges.manifestation: Shannon entropy and Boltzmann entropy are mathematically identical — information and thermodynamics are the same formalism
cross_domain_bridges.example: Compressing a file reduces its Shannon entropy the same way cooling reduces thermodynamic entropy

cross_domain_bridges.manifestation: The universe began in a low-entropy state (Big Bang) and is inexorably moving toward heat death (maximum entropy)
cross_domain_bridges.example: Black hole thermodynamics: Bekenstein-Hawking entropy S = A/4 connects gravity to thermodynamics

noise_signature.pattern: Irreversible mixing — two substances combine and cannot be separated without work
noise_signature.diagnosis: Entropy has increased; the system has moved toward equilibrium and information about initial state is lost

noise_signature.pattern: Thermal runaway — temperature increases accelerate reactions that further increase temperature
noise_signature.diagnosis: Positive feedback loop; the system has left the linear regime where small perturbations damp out

noise_signature.pattern: Heat death of subsystem — all gradients equalize, no more useful work extractable
noise_signature.diagnosis: Local equilibrium reached; differentiation has collapsed; the engine has stopped
